questions.questionText: Q1. Choose the right option. int* x, y;
options: A) x is a pointer to a integer, y is a integer
B) y is a pointer to a integer, x is a integer
C) both x and y are pointers to integer types
D) y is a pointer to a integer

questions.questionText: Q2. What is the primary operation used to add an element to a stack?
Q3. What is the principle followed by a Stack data structure?
Q4. What is the principle followed by a queue data structure?
Q5. Which operation is used to remove an element from a queue?
Q6. What happens if you try to pop an element from an empty stack?
options: A) The program enters an infinite loop.
C) The stack automatically resizes to prevent an error.

questions.questionText: Q7. Which of the following is/are linear Data Structures?
Q8. What is the output of the following program? #include <iostream> using namespace std; int main() { int arr[] = {4, 5, 6, 7}; int* p = (arr + 2); cout << *arr + 11; return 0; }
Q9. What is the output of the following program? #include <iostream> using namespace std; int main() { char *ptr; char Str[] = "abcdefg"; ptr = Str; ptr += 5; cout << ptr; return 0; }
